channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇨🇳 China arrests US scholar of Myanmar on spying suspicion
item.description: China confirmed the arrest of Min Zin, a US citizen who leads a think-tank focused on Myanmar, on suspicion of spying and endangering national security. The detention raises tensions between Washington and Beijing.

item.title: 🇦🇪 UAE to unlock frozen Iranian funds amid US ceasefire push
item.description: The United Arab Emirates agreed to unlock billions of dollars for Iran in a tactical shift after weeks of Iranian pressure. The move comes as US-Iran peace negotiations appear close to agreement.

item.title: 📺 Paramount-Warner merger cleared by DOJ antitrust review
item.description: The US Justice Department determined that Paramount Skydance's proposed acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery is unlikely to harm competition or consumers, clearing a major regulatory hurdle for the media megamerger.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine requests $20 billion from allies to sustain momentum
item.description: Ukraine is seeking an additional $20 billion in military funding from allies to cement its battlefield advantage over Russia. The request comes as Kyiv confirms strikes on Russian refineries and Putin announces satellite-based drone control systems.

item.title: ⛪ Pope Leo warns Europe's migrant traffickers to repent or face hell
item.description: Pope Leo issued a stern warning to human traffickers exploiting desperate migrants trying to reach Europe through Spain's Canary Islands, telling them to repent or face divine judgment.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Iran in limbo between war and peace as deal talks intensify
item.description: Since announcing a nominal cease-fire two months ago, Iran, Israel and the US have remained locked in low-intensity conflict while diplomats work toward a lasting agreement. The outcome remains uncertain.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran loosens Strait of Hormuz stranglehold amid ceasefire talks
item.description: Iran has reduced its naval presence in the strategic Strait of Hormuz as US-Iran peace negotiations progress. The waterway remains a critical chokepoint for global oil shipments.

item.title: 🇪🇺 NATO faces test as US and Europe grapple with defense priorities
item.description: European allies are reassessing their defense posture as the US navigates multiple global conflicts. The alliance's ability to respond to emerging threats remains in focus.

item.title: 🚢 Submarines increase numbers in new arms race
item.description: Naval powers are expanding submarine fleets as underwater warfare becomes a priority. The trend reflects growing strategic competition in maritime domains.

item.title: ⚔️ "Hell's Army" — migrant crisis deepens
item.description: Migrants face deadly conditions as they attempt dangerous crossings. The humanitarian crisis continues to strain resources and test international response mechanisms.

item.title: 🛰️ Astronomers fear orbital data centers will interfere with observations
item.description: Scientists warn that proposed orbital data centers could degrade astronomical observations. The tension between commercial space development and scientific research continues.

item.title: 🔍 US surveillance authorities face scrutiny amid global tensions
item.description: Congress is reconsidering surveillance powers at a time of heightened international threats. The debate balances national security needs against civil liberties concerns.

item.title: 💰 SpaceX opens trading at $150 after record-busting IPO
item.description: SpaceX stock surged on its first day of trading, making Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire. The IPO marks a milestone for the commercial space industry.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK economy contracted by 0.1% in April
item.description: British economic output declined slightly in April according to official statistics. The data adds to concerns about the UK's economic trajectory.

item.title: 🏥 US provides $50M funding to counter Ebola outbreak
item.description: The US government allocated emergency funding to combat an Ebola outbreak. Health officials are coordinating response efforts with international partners.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia building infrastructure for major troop mobilization
item.description: Reports indicate Russia is constructing facilities that could support a large-scale mobilization. The development raises concerns about escalation in the Ukraine conflict.

item.title: 🇨🇩 Violence breaks out at DRC rally against presidential term limit change
item.description: Protests turned violent in the Democratic Republic of Congo as citizens opposed efforts to extend presidential term limits. Security forces clashed with demonstrators.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU asylum pact takes effect
item.description: The European Union's new asylum regulations are now in force, marking a significant shift in how member states handle migration and refugee claims.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US deports Iranian pro-democracy activist to Central African Republic
item.description: An Iranian pro-democracy activist was deported from the US to the Central African Republic according to his lawyer. The case raises questions about US immigration enforcement.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine announces sweeping military reforms to ease manpower crisis
item.description: Ukraine unveiled major reforms to its military service terms in an effort to address a critical manpower shortage after four years of war with Russia.

item.title: 🇲🇲 Factories in Myanmar struggle with import controls
item.description: Myanmar's manufacturing sector faces challenges from new import restrictions. The controls are straining supply chains and production capacity.

item.title: 🇵🇰 Pakistan PM Sharif says final text of US-Iran peace deal agreed
item.description: Pakistan's prime minister announced that both sides have agreed on the final text of a US-Iran peace deal. The statement suggests negotiations are nearing completion.

item.title: ⚔️ Two killed, 10 injured in Ukraine attack on Russia's Bryansk region
item.description: Ukraine and Russia exchanged overnight drone strikes. Ukrainian forces hit oil refineries while Russia targeted railway stations and electrical infrastructure.

item.title: ⛪ Pope Leo warns Europe faces secular future as Spain rejects Catholic identity
item.description: Pope Leo XIV raised concerns about Europe's spiritual direction during his visit to Spain. The pontiff warned against abandoning Christian heritage.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran in limbo between war and peace as negotiations continue
item.description: The conflict between the US and Iran has entered an uncertain phase. While diplomats work toward a settlement, military tensions persist.
